name: Gen X
description: Latchkey kids, MTV, grunge, slacker culture — the generation that watched the Cold War end and the internet arrive.

questions.q: What range of birth years generally defines Generation X?
Which author popularized 'Generation X' with his 1991 novel of the same name?
Which generation immediately precedes Generation X?
Which generation immediately follows Generation X?
Which nickname for Gen X reflects kids who came home alone after school while parents worked?
Which 1980s cable network is credited with shaping Gen X's musical and cultural identity?

source.notes: MTV launched on August 1, 1981 with The Buggles' 'Video Killed the Radio Star.'

questions.q: Which 1990s rock subgenre, born in Seattle, became the soundtrack of disaffected Gen X youth?
Which 1991 Nirvana album, with the song 'Smells Like Teen Spirit', defined Gen X grunge?
Which 1994 Ben Stiller film about post-college Gen X twentysomethings popularized the 'slacker' archetype?

source.notes: Starred Winona Ryder, Ethan Hawke, Janeane Garofalo, Stiller.

questions.q: Which 1980s home-video format Gen X grew up with replaced 16mm and TV reruns for movie-watching?

source.notes: VHS won the format war over Betamax in the late 1970s.

questions.q: Which 1989 event symbolizes the Cold War's end for Gen Xers (most of whom were teens or young adults)?
Which 1985 John Hughes film became a quintessential Gen X high-school movie?

source.notes: All four are John Hughes Gen X classics; The Breakfast Club is most often cited.

questions.q: Which 1993 Chris Carter sci-fi/paranormal series epitomized Gen X paranoia?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: year fixed from 1995 to 1993 — premiered Sept 10, 1993; ran nine seasons. 'The truth is out there.'

questions.q: Which 1997 Apple campaign — 'Think ___' — was launched by a 41-year-old Gen X-defining Steve Jobs back at Apple?
Which 1995 Sandra Bullock thriller — about an early-internet identity crisis — was the highest-grossing tech-thriller of its era?
Which 1990s sitcom about six 20-something New Yorkers featured Gen Xers Joey, Ross, Rachel, Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe?
Which two musical genres dominated the 'golden age' of the mid-1980s to mid-1990s for many Gen Xers?

source.original_a: The golden age of hip hop

questions.q: What was Generation X originally called in demographic research, due to a fertility drop after the baby boom?
Which 1979 Robert Benton drama about a custody battle reflected the rising divorce rate Gen X kids witnessed?

source.notes: Won Best Picture; starred Dustin Hoffman and Meryl Streep.

questions.q: Which Gen X icon — punk-rock wedding singer turned solo star — fronted the band 'Generation X' before going solo?

source.notes: Idol fronted Generation X 1976-1981 before launching solo with 'Dancing With Myself,' 'White Wedding,' 'Rebel Yell.'
